sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen have shown decent form in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, scoring consistently (11 goals). Borussia M'gladbach, however, are struggling, with only 2 wins and 3 losses in their last 5, including a heavy 0-4 defeat, and they’ve failed to score in 3 of those games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Leverkusen have dominated recent encounters against M'gladbach, winning both of the last two head-to-head matches (3-1 and 3-2). They’ve scored 6 goals across these games, while M'gladbach managed 3, indicating Leverkusen’s attacking edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen rank higher in the league (12th vs 17th) and have better offensive and defensive stats, scoring 7 goals and conceding 6 this season. M'gladbach have struggled massively, failing to score in multiple games and conceding heavily (5 goals in 3 matches).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Leverkusen’s Patrik Schick and Alejandro Grimaldo are in form, contributing 8 goals combined in recent matches. M'gladbach lack standout performers, and their defensive frailty could be exploited by Leverkusen’s attacking prowess.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains level at 0-0 between Bayer 04 Leverkusen and Borussia M'gladbach at the BayArena. Leverkusen have dominated possession and created more entries into the final third, showing intent to control the game. However, neither team has managed to convert their opportunities, with both sides missing a big chance each. The game has been tightly contested, with a few disciplinary issues arising as evidenced by the yellow cards shown to players on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen have shown significant control in the first half, holding the lion's share of possession and generating more shots on target. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area demonstrate a strong attacking presence, though they’ve struggled to capitalize on key opportunities. Players like Alejandro Grimaldo and Eliesse Ben Seghir have been pivotal in creating chances, while Patrik Schick will be disappointed to have missed a big chance. Defensively, they’ve held up reasonably well but need to tighten up to prevent counter-attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Borussia M'gladbach have been on the back foot for much of the first half, with significantly less possession and fewer entries into the final third. Despite this, they’ve managed to create a big chance and have been more effective in aerial duels and overall duels won. Their defense, led by Nico Elvedi, has been solid with several clearances to keep the scoreline level. However, they’ll need to push forward more aggressively in the second half if they hope to turn the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Leverkusen dictate the tempo early on with consistent attacking pressure and sustained spells in the opposition half. Their momentum was particularly strong in the opening stages and towards the middle of the half, where they mounted several dangerous attacks. M'gladbach, on the other hand, had moments of resurgence, especially around the midway point and towards the end of the half, with brief but notable shifts in momentum that saw them threaten on the counter. Both teams alternated control at times, but Leverkusen generally held the upper hand in pushing forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Leverkusen, converting their dominance into goals will be crucial in the second half. They need to be more clinical in front of goal, especially after missing key opportunities. M'gladbach must focus on capitalizing on counter-attacks and maintaining defensive solidity while looking to exploit any lapses in Leverkusen’s backline. Discipline will also be a factor, as both teams have picked up cards, and avoiding further bookings or a potential red card will be important to maintain a full squad on the pitch.
